phonetic: 藞s瑟nfj蕣蓹l; s瑟n藞fju藧蓹l
examples: The Air Force already has certified the synfuel blend for use on the B-52 bomber and expected to complete certification for the C-17 fleet early this year.
Now dress a synfuel subsidy in a pair of overalls and you have something irresistible to a politician.
Petroleum price crashes have happened before, turning synfuel projects into rust buckets.
That synfuel would keep Germany going during World War II.
